exam.name: English Language Arts: Content Knowledge
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for prospective secondary English teachers with syllabus-mapped coverage across reading, language and vocabulary, and writing, speaking, and listening.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis 5038 with ELA content knowledge you can apply on test day
exam.hero_subheadline: Work through every domain in focused 15-minute topics aligned to the official ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis ELA prep scatters practice without a clear map of what the exam actually weights—reading, conventions, and communication skills blur together.
exam.companion_solution: We translate the official ETS syllabus into a complete topic map with retention-focused notes and consistent coverage of literature, informational text, and language use.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring secondary English teacher building deep content knowledge for literature, informational texts, language conventions, and communication. Responds to passage-based reasoning, grammar-in-context items, and writing- and speaking-focused scenarios.

sections.title: Reading
sections.description: Study of literature (stories, drama, poetry) and informational texts (literary nonfiction, essays, biographies, speeches).

topics.title: Major Works and Authors of Literature
topics.description: Familiarity with prominent works and authors across United States, British, World, and Young Adult literature.
learning_objectives: Identify the authors and titles of major works of fiction, poetry, drama, and literary nonfiction.

topics.title: Historical, Cultural, and Literary Contexts
topics.description: Understanding the context of major literary works to enhance reading comprehension and analysis.
learning_objectives: Identify the historical, cultural, or literary context of major works of fiction, poetry, drama, and literary nonfiction.

topics.title: Characteristics of Primary Literary Genres
topics.description: Identifying typical features of genres and applying correct terminology.
learning_objectives: Identify typical characteristics of a genre.
Apply correct terminology for a genre (e.g., stanza versus paragraph).

topics.title: Characteristics of Major Literary Forms
topics.description: Distinguishing major forms within genres through structural and content differences.
learning_objectives: Identify characteristics of major forms within each genre through distinctions in structure and content (e.g., sonnets versus ballads, satire versus realism).

topics.title: Textual Evidence and Interpretations of Literary Texts
topics.description: Using text effectively to deduce meaning, both literal and figurative, and support literary analysis.
learning_objectives: Comprehend the literal and figurative meanings of a text.
Draw inferences from a text.
Determine the textual evidence that supports an analysis of a literary text.

topics.title: Developing Themes in Literature
topics.description: Analyzing how central ideas and themes are developed, including universal themes and allusions.
learning_objectives: Identify the theme(s) or central idea(s) of a given text.
Analyze how a theme or central idea is developed throughout one or more works.
Recognize universal themes from myths, traditional stories, or religious works and how they are rendered or alluded to.

topics.title: Literary Elements and Textual Meaning
topics.description: Understanding how plot, point of view, tone, characterization, and setting shape the narrative.
learning_objectives: Analyze the impact of differences in the points of view of characters and/or narrators.
Analyze the structure of a plot.
Analyze how different elements contribute to mood, tone, and conflict.
Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or story events impact meaning.
Analyze the text for character development.

topics.title: Figurative Language and its Effect
topics.description: Identifying and interpreting metaphor, hyperbole, imagery, and other rhetorical figures in literature.
learning_objectives: Identify examples of various types of figurative language (e.g., extended metaphor, imagery, hyperbole).
Interpret figurative language in context and analyze its role in the text.

topics.title: Poetic Devices and Structure
topics.description: Analyzing how elements like rhythm, rhyme scheme, and stanza format contribute to a poem's overall effect.
learning_objectives: Analyze how poetic devices (e.g., rhyme scheme, rhythm, figurative language) contribute to meaning in a poem.
Analyze how structure (e.g., stanza, free verse, concrete poem) contributes to meaning in a poem.

topics.title: Reading Strategies for Comprehension
topics.description: Using and evaluating predictions, summaries, and text connections to support active reading.
learning_objectives: Identify literacy skills to support active reading (e.g., text-to-self connection, prediction, summarizing).
Evaluate a summary of a passage.
Evaluate the strength of a prediction based on textual evidence.

topics.title: Research-Based Strategies for Reading Instruction
topics.description: Applying educational research to classroom practices for teaching reading.
learning_objectives: Recognize commonly used research-based strategies for teaching reading (e.g., activating prior knowledge, modeling metacognitive practices).
Evaluate the effectiveness of specific strategies to support a particular reading task.
Interpret research and apply it to particular reading instruction challenges.

topics.title: Literary Theories for ELA Educators
topics.description: Familiarity with major critical theories used to interpret and critique literature.
learning_objectives: Recognize ways literary theories (e.g., reader-response, feminist criticism) are used to interpret and critique texts.

topics.title: Textual Evidence and Interpretations of Informational Texts
topics.description: Evaluating non-fiction texts by drawing inferences and citing textual evidence.
learning_objectives: Comprehend literal and figurative meanings of an informational text.
Draw inferences from an informational text.
Determine the textual evidence that supports an analysis of an informational text.

topics.title: Organizational Patterns and Text Structures
topics.description: Understanding how organizational frameworks build the central idea in informational reading.
learning_objectives: Identify the central idea of an informational text.
Analyze how an author develops or refines a central idea.
Identify the organizational pattern (e.g., problem-solution, cause-effect, sequence).
Analyze how ideas are connected and distinguished.
Identify how text features (e.g., index, glossary, headings) contribute to the central idea.

topics.title: Word Choice, Connotation, and Denotation
topics.description: Analyzing vocabulary choices, technical language, and implicit vs explicit text meanings.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between connotation and denotation.
Identify how technical language is used.
Distinguish between what the text says explicitly and what may be inferred.

topics.title: Rhetorical Strategies, Purpose, and Perspective
topics.description: Evaluating how authors use rhetoric such as satire, irony, and hyperbole to convey their point of view.
learning_objectives: Determine an author's point of view or purpose.
Analyze how an author uses rhetoric to support point of view and/or purpose.
Recognize rhetorical strategies (e.g., satire, irony, understatement, hyperbole).

topics.title: Methods of Appeal and Persuasion
topics.description: Identifying and evaluating the effectiveness of persuasive techniques targeted at specific audiences.
learning_objectives: Identify methods of appeal or persuasion (e.g., expert opinion, generalization, testimonial).
Evaluate the effectiveness of an author's methods of appeal.
Understand how technical or non-technical language is used to appeal to a targeted audience.

topics.title: Developing and Supporting a Written Argument
topics.description: Critiquing arguments, examining evidence validity, and identifying logical fallacies.
learning_objectives: Evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text.
Determine an author's purpose and evaluate reasoning.
Evaluate whether evidence is relevant, factual, and/or sufficient.
Identify false statements and fallacious reasoning (e.g., slippery slope, red herring, strawman).

topics.title: Interpreting Media and Non-Print Texts
topics.description: Evaluating information across diverse media formats and recognizing inherent persuasive techniques.
learning_objectives: Evaluate multiple sources of information presented in different media or formats.
Determine persuasive techniques used in different media.

sections.title: Language Use and Vocabulary
sections.description: Understanding conventions of standard English, vocabulary development, dialect variations, and language acquisition strategies.

topics.title: Standard English Grammar and Mechanics
topics.description: Identifying parts of speech and mechanics while correcting grammatical errors.
learning_objectives: Explain the function of different parts of speech.
Identify errors in standard English grammar, usage, syntax, and mechanics.
Justify grammar, usage, syntax, and mechanics choices (e.g., colon versus semicolon).

topics.title: Usage and Syntax in Standard English
topics.description: Recognizing sentence structures and components for accurate English usage.
learning_objectives: Identify different components of sentences (e.g., clauses, phrases).
Identify different structures of sentences (e.g., simple, complex, compound).

topics.title: Affixes, Context, and Syntax for Word Meaning
topics.description: Strategies to determine the meaning of unknown words utilizing structural analysis and context clues.
learning_objectives: Apply knowledge of affixes to determine word meaning.
Use context clues to determine word meaning.
Apply knowledge of syntax to determine word meaning.

topics.title: Nuances of Word Meaning and Figures of Speech
topics.description: Interpreting word subtleties and examining figurative expressions within standard English contexts.
learning_objectives: Analyze nuances of word meaning.
Analyze figures of speech in linguistic contexts.

topics.title: Print and Digital Reference Materials
topics.description: Selecting appropriate tools like dictionaries, glossaries, and style manuals to enhance language usage.
learning_objectives: Determine the most appropriate print or digital reference material (spell checker, style manual, dictionary, glossary) for a particular language usage task.

topics.title: Variations in Dialect and Diction
topics.description: Understanding linguistic diversity across regions, times, and cultures, and recognizing appropriate contexts.
learning_objectives: Identify variation in dialect and diction across regions, cultural groups, and time periods.
Understand the concept of dialect and its appropriateness depending on purpose and audience.

topics.title: Research-Based Approaches for Language Acquisition
topics.description: Familiarity with tested strategies to assist diverse learners in acquiring standard English language skills.
learning_objectives: Recognize examples of commonly used research-based strategies for language acquisition.
Evaluate the effectiveness of specific strategies to support language acquisition.

topics.title: Supporting Vocabulary Development for Diverse Learners
topics.description: Instructional methods addressing particular challenges in building student vocabulary.
learning_objectives: Evaluate the effectiveness of specific strategies to support vocabulary development.
Interpret research and apply it to particular instructional challenges related to language acquisition and vocabulary development.

sections.title: Writing, Speaking, and Listening
sections.description: Mastery of writing modes, research practices, effective presentations, digital media, assessment methods, and inclusive ELA practices.

topics.title: Characteristics of Various Writing Modes
topics.description: Identifying the distinctions between argumentative, informative, and narrative writing formats.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between common modes of writing (e.g., argumentative, informative/explanatory, narrative).
Identify examples of common types within modes of writing (e.g., journal, letter, essay, speech).
Determine which mode is the most appropriate for an author's purpose and audience.

topics.title: Task, Purpose, and Audience in Writing
topics.description: Understanding how situational awareness shapes effective writing choices.
learning_objectives: Identify how the task, purpose, or intended audience affects a piece of writing.
Choose the most appropriate type of writing for a task, purpose, and audience.
Evaluate the effectiveness of a piece of writing for a specific task, purpose, and audience.

topics.title: Characteristics of Clear and Coherent Writing
topics.description: Organizing, transitioning, and applying stylistic choices to produce structurally sound text.
learning_objectives: Identify details that develop a main idea.
Organize a text clearly and coherently with varied and effective transitions.
Justify stylistic choices within a piece of writing.
Introduce, develop, and conclude a text effectively.

topics.title: Effective and Ethical Research Practices
topics.description: Evaluating credibility of sources and implementing correct citation mechanisms.
learning_objectives: Identify relevant information during research on a given topic.
Evaluate the credibility of a print or digital source.
Identify effective research practices (e.g., formulating a question, narrowing a topic).
Identify the components of a citation and cite source material appropriately.

topics.title: Components of Effective Speech and Presentation Delivery
topics.description: Techniques for eye contact, visual aids, tone, and logical presentation of information.
learning_objectives: Identify characteristics of effective delivery of a speech or presentation.
Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different media to present ideas.
Determine whether information is presented clearly, concisely, and logically.

topics.title: Effective Use of Digital Media in ELA
topics.description: Instructional methods for adopting technological tools like blogs, wikis, and presentation software.
learning_objectives: Identify techniques for instructing students to choose and use technological tools for effective communication.
Evaluate the effectiveness of specific technology-based strategies to achieve enhanced understanding of communication goals.

topics.title: Research-Based Approaches to Teaching Writing
topics.description: Identifying and applying strategies such as systematic instruction and modeling in the ELA classroom.
learning_objectives: Recognize commonly used research-based strategies (e.g., explicit, systematic writing instruction; modeling) for teaching components of writing.
Identify research-based strategies for teaching particular writing tasks.
Interpret research and apply it to particular writing instruction challenges.

topics.title: Assessing Reading, Writing, Speaking, and Listening
topics.description: Methods and effectiveness of formative and summative assessments, including rubrics and conferring.
learning_objectives: Recognize a variety of research-based approaches to and purposes of formative and summative assessment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of approaches to assessment of reading, writing, speaking, and listening (e.g., use of rubrics, conferencing techniques).

topics.title: Components of Effective Oral Communication
topics.description: Promoting productive participation and active listening in group and individual settings.
learning_objectives: Identify a variety of techniques (e.g., selecting age-appropriate topics, ensuring accountability) to ensure productive participation and active listening in collaborative discussions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of specific strategies for students initiating and participating effectively in discussions.

topics.title: Diverse Perspectives and Inclusive Instruction
topics.description: Utilizing knowledge of students' backgrounds, identities, and cultures to create responsive curriculum.
learning_objectives: Use knowledge of students' individual and group identities to plan instruction responsive to their needs.
Know strategies for creating a safe environment for reading, writing, speaking, and listening to take place.
